chunk_summary: This section outlines a staged approach to implementing tele-nutrition systems, emphasizing workflow mapping, safety protocols, data quality, and population management to ensure effective care delivery and avoid common pitfalls.
learning_objectives: Understand the staged implementation process for tele-nutrition systems, from triage to dashboard operations.
Identify key safety protocols and data quality measures for tele-nutrition workflows.
Learn strategies to prevent implementation pitfalls, such as misclassification and workflow mismatches.
Explore methods for scaling tele-nutrition systems after pilot testing.
key_facts: Tele-nutrition implementation involves four stages: safety, data quality, longitudinal reliability, and population management.
Stage 1 includes triage scripts, red-flag pathways, consent templates, and escalation directories.
Stage 2 focuses on data quality with coached anthropometry protocols and standardized counseling scripts for high-burden issues like undernutrition and obesity.
Stage 3 requires a registry to track risk tiers and follow-up dates, ensuring outreach for overdue high-risk children.
Stage 4 involves dashboards, alert rules, and monthly quality improvement cycles using outcome checkpoints.
A one-week pilot with 10 families is recommended to identify workflow failures before scaling.
reference_quote: Buying software before mapping the workflow leads to 'data without care,' where measurements are collected but follow-up fails.
